title: Semantik Architect Frame Schema — Relational: Ownership / Control
description: Canonical semantic frame for ownership, control, operation, or management relations between two entities. This is a frame schema, not a planner/runtime payload. Entity references are shared-contract refs; the frame itself remains backend-agnostic.

frame_type.description: Canonical frame type. Authored JSON should prefer "relational.ownership_control"; legacy aliases remain accepted for compatibility.

frame_family.description: Optional high-level family label for indexing or diagnostics.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this relation instance, such as an internal row ID, statement cluster ID, or normalized fact ID.

owner.description: The owning, controlling, operating, or managing entity.

asset.description: The entity that is owned, controlled, operated, or managed by the owner.

relation_type.description: Coarse semantic label for the relation, for example: ownership, majority_ownership, minority_ownership, control, operation, management, joint_control.

control_basis.description: Optional description of how control is exercised, for example: equity, voting_rights, contractual, statutory, operational, beneficial.

ownership_share_pct.description: Optional approximate ownership share as a percentage from 0 to 100.

voting_rights_pct.description: Optional voting-rights share as a percentage from 0 to 100.

economic_interest_pct.description: Optional economic-interest share as a percentage from 0 to 100.

control_level.description: Optional qualitative description of control level, for example: full, majority, minority, joint, indirect, operational_only, de_facto, de_jure.

time_span.description: Optional coarse time span during which the ownership / control relation holds.

jurisdiction.description: Optional jurisdiction in which the ownership / control relation is especially relevant, typically a country, region, or legal domain entity.

status.description: Optional status label such as current, former, pending, disputed, dissolved.

source_id.description: Optional upstream source or statement identifier, such as a Wikidata statement ID.

attributes.description: Free-form attribute bag for non-core extensibility, for example: listing_status, beneficial_owner_type, voting_arrangement, holding_structure, board_control_notes.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata preserved through normalization for traceability or migration compatibility.

TimeSpan.description: Coarse time span representation used in frame schemas when no standalone shared time-span contract is available.

approximate.description: Whether the date or span is approximate, inferred, or fuzzy.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata related to the time span.
